name: Dense Tier-2 LoRA — same-repo HARD in-batch negatives (issue_to_fixing_pr)
hypothesis: R16D showed the Tier-2 LoRA win is rank-saturated with harder/more in-batch negatives the only lever. Does making negatives HARDER (repo-homogeneous batches) beat making them MORE (bigger random pool) at matched pool size?
command: PYTORCH_ENABLE_MPS_FALLBACK=1 python data/tier2/run_negatives_sweep.py && PYTHONPATH=src python data/tier2/run_negatives_bootstrap.py
leakage_checks: LoRA trains on train-repo fixes pairs only; eval on 32 held-out test repos; references scrubbed.
Bootstrap CI (query + repo-cluster) over the 1,171 held-out queries excludes zero: dR@1 +0.1375, CI [+0.112, +0.164]; 31/32 repos improve, 0 regress.
known_limitations: Trained on Apple M5 GPU (MPS); MPS is NOT bit-deterministic (run-to-run ~+/-0.008 R@1; this cell read +0.146 on a first run, +0.1375 on the clean re-run the saved embeddings + CI use).
Single cross-repo split; single seed. The hardness gain over matched random (+0.017) is ~2x the MPS run-to-run noise, so multi-seed confirmation is the natural follow-up.
Larger random pools (b192/b384) OOM the M5 MPS allocator; practical in-batch pool ceiling ~b96. Exploratory.
